name: Kung Po Chicken
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut chicken into 1/2 inch cubes. You will have about 1-1/2 cups.
2. Combine the egg white, cornstarch, soy sauce, and 1 tablespoon water in a bowl. Add to the chicken cubes and mix well. Set aside for 30 minutes.
3. Wipe chili peppers clean with a wet towel. Remove the tops and set aside on a plate with scallions.
4. Combine the sauce ingredients in a cup. Set aside near the cooking area.
5. Place a strainer over a pot near the cooking area.
6. Heat a wok over moderate heat until it is very hot. Add peanut oil and heat to about 300°F.
7. Mix the chicken again and add to the hot oil. Raise the heat to high and stir the chicken until it separates or until most of the chicken has changed color but is not yet completely cooked.
8. Pour the oil and chicken into the strainer to drain. As soon as the oil has drained away, transfer the chicken to a plate.
9. Heat the same wok. Add 2 tablespoons of the drained oil, brown the chili peppers until they turn dark red, then add the scallions and the cooked chicken and stir fry for 1 minute.
10. Stir the sauce mixture well and add it to the chicken while stirring over high heat until it thickens and coats the chicken with a clear glaze. Add the peanuts and mix well.
